data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $50.4794 million yesterday, with only BlackRock ETHB experiencing a net inflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $50.4794 million yesterday (April 27th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $11.7602 million. ETHB's historical total net inflow has reached $44.0122 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $48.4315 million. FETH's historical total net inflow has reached $2.291 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $13.532 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.89%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $12.048 billion.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: More focused on Warsh's balance sheet stance than on the short-term interest rate path
data.description: At the Bitcoin 2026 conference, Arthur Hayes stated that he is more concerned about the statements made by Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh regarding the balance sheet than the short-term interest rate trend, as this will affect dollar liquidity and the pricing of risky assets such as Bitcoin. Hayes believes that the market's expectation of Warsh being "hawkish" may be over-interpreted, and he anticipates that the Federal Reserve is more likely to adjust liquidity through asset swaps and other means, rather than weakening the US Treasury's debt issuance capacity.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Maintains Rates Amid Hawkish Outlook
data.description: On April 28, according to Jin10, Marcel Thieliant, Head of Asia Economics at Capital Economics, stated that despite the Bank of Japan keeping interest rates unchanged, its outlook report leans hawkish. Thieliant maintains his forecast that the Bank of Japan will raise rates in June. He added that three members voted in favor of a rate hike, marking the largest dissent since the negative interest rate policy was implemented in 2016. While it was not surprising that consistently hawkish members Takata Hajime and Tamura Naoki opposed, Junko Nakagawa joined the dissenting camp for the first time.

data.title: Abu Dhabi's ADNOC Plans Major U.S. Investment in Natural Gas Sector
data.description:
Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) is planning to invest billions of dollars in the U.S. to develop its natural gas business, according to the Financial Times. The company's overseas investment division, XRG, led by newly appointed Chief Investment Officer Nameer Siddiqui, is evaluating 29 potential deals to establish a vertically integrated global natural gas operation. This initiative may encompass all stages from gas extraction, pipeline and processing plant ownership, overseas transportation, to re-gasification facilities and pipelines to end-users in destination countries.

Siddiqui stated that ADNOC is considering various investment models, including controlling stakes, drilling joint ventures, and minority equity positions, to cater to the growing global demand for liquefied natural gas and to meet the U.S. natural gas needs for powering data centers. When questioned about XRG's financial capacity amid challenges posed by the Iran conflict, Siddiqui emphasized the company's unwavering commitment to investing billions in the U.S. energy value chain, contingent upon achieving suitable return expectations.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations could lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, Coinglass data indicates that if Ethereum surpasses $2,404, the cumulative short liquidation intensity on these exchanges could reach $1.093 billion. Conversely, if Ethereum falls below $2,176, the cumulative long liquidation intensity could amount to $697 million.

data.title: LianLian Digital Acquires MSB License in Canada
data.description: LianLian Digital has successfully acquired a Money Services Business (MSB) license through the purchase of an entity in Canada, according to ChainCatcher. This license, granted by the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (FINTRAC), allows the company to engage in regulated activities such as foreign exchange, remittance, virtual currency, and payment services. The specific acquisition amount has not been disclosed.

data.title: The nine-day streak of net inflows ended yesterday, with Bitcoin spot ETFs experiencing a total net outflow of $263 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $263 million yesterday (April 27th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net outflow of $150 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $10.883 billion. This was followed by Grayscale ETF (GBTC), with a single-day net outflow of $46.6259 million. GBTC's historical total net outflow has reached $26.262 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $101.234 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.57%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $58.301 billion.

data.title: BOJ's Dissenting Votes Influence Yen Appreciation, Says Former Official
data.description:
Fukuoka Financial Group's Chief Strategist and former Bank of Japan (BOJ) official, Tohru Sasaki, has attributed the recent appreciation of the yen to three dissenting votes within the BOJ. According to Jin10, the market's focus is on the Consumer Price Index (CPI), and most BOJ members have raised their inflation expectations, which is another reason the decision is seen as hawkish.

One of the dissenting members, Junko Nakagawa, will see her term end in June, and she will be replaced by Ayano Sato, who is considered very dovish. This change suggests that this might be the last time three dissenting members appear simultaneously. However, Sasaki emphasized that three votes are still three votes, leading him to view the outcome as hawkish.

data.title: STRC Perpetual Preferred Shares: Impact on Bitcoin Buying Pressure
data.description: STRC perpetual preferred shares have emerged as a financial tool that converts fixed income demand into buying pressure for Bitcoin. According to PANews, this instrument offers an annualized return of 11.5% and has shown notable performance during bull markets. However, it also faces asymmetric credit risk when Bitcoin prices decline. The mechanism behind STRC perpetual preferred shares involves leveraging fixed income investments to generate interest, which is then used to purchase Bitcoin, thereby increasing demand for the cryptocurrency. This approach has attracted investors seeking high returns, especially during periods of market optimism. Despite its potential benefits, the instrument's reliance on Bitcoin's price stability poses a risk. In bearish market conditions, the credit risk associated with STRC perpetual preferred shares becomes more pronounced, as the value of the underlying Bitcoin assets may decrease, impacting the overall return. Investors are advised to consider these factors when evaluating the potential of STRC perpetual preferred shares as part of their investment strategy.

data.title: The ZetaChain vulnerability was exploited, possibly stemming from a flaw in the GatewayZEVM call function.
data.description: SlowMist reported that ZetaChain was attacked and exploited. Preliminary analysis indicates the vulnerability stems from a lack of access control and input validation in the GatewayZEVM contract's `call` function. Attackers could exploit this to initiate malicious cross-chain calls and perform arbitrary operations on the target chain to transfer funds via the relay mechanism. SlowMist stated that attackers forged cross-chain events to trigger malicious calls to the relay, thereby stealing funds. The related attack transactions have been disclosed.

data.title: ZachXBT questions whether Sam Altman's associated project, World, is using token distribution to obtain biometric data from users in low-income countries.
data.description: achXBT published an article on the X platform criticizing Sam Altman's associated project World (formerly WorldCoin), questioning its practice of exchanging WLD tokens for biometric data from users in low-income countries. The article also criticized WLD's low-circulation token design, claiming that its identity verification system fosters black market trading of verified accounts, and questioned the token inflation mechanism and off-exchange holdings.

data.title: ZetaChain Exploited Due to Contract Vulnerability
data.description:
ZetaChain has been exploited, according to Odaily, with initial analysis indicating that the vulnerability stems from the GatewayZEVM contract's call function lacking access control and input validation. This flaw allowed attackers to initiate malicious cross-chain calls and execute arbitrary operations on the target chain to transfer funds.

The attackers reportedly forged cross-chain events to trigger relayers to execute malicious calls, resulting in the theft of funds. The transactions related to this attack have now been disclosed.

data.title: Japan Considers 'Golden Share' Plan for Tokyo Electric Power Restructuring
data.description: The Japanese government is contemplating the introduction of a 'golden share' plan as part of the restructuring of Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO), according to Tokyo Television. This move aims to provide the government with special rights in the company, potentially influencing key decisions and ensuring national interests are safeguarded. The plan is still under consideration, and further details have yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Ethena Set to Unlock 171.88 Million Tokens on May 5
data.description: Ethena (ENA) is scheduled to unlock approximately 171.88 million tokens on May 5 at 0:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is valued at around $18.27 million. The data comes from Web3 asset data platform RootData, which tracks token unlock schedules.

data.title: Bank of Japan: Careful Examination Needed for Core Inflation Stability Around 2%
data.description: The Bank of Japan has emphasized the necessity of closely examining whether core inflation is stabilizing around the 2% mark. According to Jin10, this statement reflects the central bank's ongoing assessment of inflationary trends and their implications for monetary policy. The Bank of Japan continues to monitor economic indicators to determine the appropriate course of action in maintaining price stability.

data.title: BOJ Quarterly Report: Consumer Inflation May Accelerate Gradually
data.description: The Bank of Japan's quarterly report suggests that potential consumer inflation could gradually accelerate. According to Jin10, the report indicates that inflation may converge towards levels consistent with the price target between the second half of the 2026 fiscal year and the 2027 fiscal year.

data.title: Bank of Japan Warns of Potential Food Price Risks Due to Rising Raw Material Costs
data.description: The Bank of Japan has highlighted concerns regarding food prices, which may exceed expectations due to increasing costs in the raw materials market. According to Jin10, the central bank emphasized the need to monitor these developments closely, as they could impact inflation and economic stability. The warning comes amid broader discussions on global economic trends and the challenges posed by fluctuating commodity prices.

data.title: A whale deposited $3.25 million worth of ASTER funds into Aster.
data.description: According to Odaily Planet Daily, Lookonchain monitoring indicates that a whale withdrew 10 million ASTER tokens (worth $6.71 million) from Binance three days ago and has now deposited 5 million ASTER tokens, worth approximately $3.25 million, into Aster.

data.title: Bank of Japan Revises GDP Growth Forecasts for 2026-2028
data.description: The Bank of Japan has updated its GDP growth forecasts for the fiscal years 2026 to 2028. According to Jin10, the central bank now expects the real GDP growth rates to be 0.5% in 2026, 0.7% in 2027, and 0.8% in 2028. These figures represent a revision from the previous forecasts made in January 2026 and 2027, which were 1.0% and 0.8%, respectively.

data.title: Bank of Japan: Economic Outlook Faces Downside Risks
data.description: The Bank of Japan has indicated that the country's economic outlook is facing downside risks. According to Jin10, the central bank also noted that the inflation outlook is subject to upside risks. These assessments reflect ongoing uncertainties in the global and domestic economic environment, which could impact Japan's economic recovery and inflation trajectory.
